name: BeagleV-Ahead
description: Open-source RISC-V SBC built around the T-Head TH1520 with BeagleBone-compatible cape headers

release-date.comment: Announced and went on sale July 12, 2023.

status.comment: Still listed as in-stock at Seeed (China warehouse) and at major distributors (Newark/Farnell) as of 2026-04-26.

open-source-hardware.comment: OSHWA certified UID US002535; PCB EDA files and schematics published under CC BY 4.0.

country-of-origin.comment: BeagleBoard.org Foundation is a Michigan, USA-based 501(c)(3); SoC is from China-based T-Head and assembly is via Seeed in China.

cpu-cores.comment: Four Xuantie C910 application cores. The TH1520 also includes a Xuantie C906 audio DSP and a low-power Xuantie E902 core, but those are not general-purpose application cores.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: Vendor product page advertises 2 GHz; documentation lists sustained 1.85 GHz. Using the more conservative documented value per Assessment Guidelines (highest sustained stock clock).

process-node.comment: T-Head does not publish the TH1520 process node and reliable third-party identification of the foundry/node was not found.

gpu.comment: Vendor product page cites 50 GFLOPS, 3 Mpixel/s for the BXM-4-64.

vulkan-support.comment: Vulkan 1.2 supported by the Imagination BXM-4-64; as of late 2025 the mainline drm/imagination driver brings hardware-accelerated 3D on this GPU on RISC-V (TH1520 became the first RISC-V SoC with mainline accelerated 3D).

npu-performance-tops.comment: Vendor-quoted 4 TOPS @ INT8 at 1 GHz (TH1520 NPU). Not directly comparable across vendors.

ram-min-gb.comment: Single SKU; not sold in alternative RAM configurations.

ethernet.comment: Realtek RTL8211F-VD-CG Gigabit PHY, single RJ-45.

bluetooth.comment: Bluetooth 5.2 via the AP6203BM combo module.

usb-a-3x-ports.comment: No USB-A. The board has a single micro-USB 3.0 OTG port (5 Gbps).

usb-c-ports-data.comment: USB connector is micro-USB 3.0 (micro-AB), not USB-C.

max-display-resolution.value: 4K@75 (decode); display output untested at this resolution
max-display-resolution.comment: Vendor cites 4Kp75 H.265/H.264 decode. The exact maximum micro-HDMI display output resolution is not separately advertised on the product page.

audio-jack.comment: 3.5 mm analog audio jack present per LinuxGizmos breakdown of the board.

gpio-pins.comment: P8 + P9 BeagleBone-style cape headers, 46 pins each (92 total). Header is 3.3 V only — unlike the BBB which offered 5 V on P9. Also includes a separate mikroBUS shuttle connector.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: Uses BeagleBone P8/P9 layout, not Raspberry Pi 40-pin pinout.

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: TH1520 has internal PCIe but no PCIe (M.2 or otherwise) is brought out to the user on this board.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I2C/SPI/UART/PWM/ADC/GPIO available via P8/P9 cape headers and the mikroBUS shuttle connector.

power-input.value: 5V DC via barrel jack or micro-USB 3.0

idle-power-w.comment: No measured idle figure from a credible review (Phoronix/CNX/Jeff Geerling) found. Per Notes-for-Researchers #8, leaving null rather than guessing from TDP.

load-power-w.comment: No measured load figure from a credible review found. Vendor does not publish typical full-load draw.

cooling-required.comment: Vendor ships the board bare; no fan or active cooler is specified or required, but the SoC needs a heatsink under sustained load. Treating as heatsink-recommended on that basis (no explicit vendor cooling guidance for sustained max load).

rtc-battery-header.comment: Neither the official product page, the BeagleBoard documentation index, nor the design specs page explicitly confirm or deny an RTC battery holder; could not verify from primary sources.

length-mm.comment: Official PCB size 96.5 × 60.5 × 1.6 mm per the Design & Specifications page (CNX-Software's earlier 86.4 × 53.4 figure was incorrect).

form-factor-class.comment: Larger than the BeagleBone Black footprint (BBB is 86.4 × 53.4 mm; BeagleV-Ahead is 96.5 × 60.5 mm), so it is not strictly a BBB-class form factor, even though the cape headers (P8/P9) are pin-compatible.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Mounting holes do not match Pi-standard. Could not confirm exact match to BeagleBone Black mounting pattern from primary sources.

weight-g.comment: Net (bare board) weight per official design page; 128.8 g is gross (boxed) weight.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Mainline upstreaming has progressed substantially since 2023: TH1520 SoC support, eMMC/microSD, Ethernet, USB are upstream. Linux 6.17 added the GPU power-sequencing driver and the drm/imagination GPU driver gives mainline 3D acceleration on RISC-V (a first). Display controller (Verisilicon DC8200) was still in review (v4) as of late 2025; NPU has no mainline driver. Mainline-kernel coverage is meaningful but not complete — classifying as partial.

official-os-image.comment: BeagleBoard.org publishes an official Ubuntu image and a Yocto image for the BeagleV-Ahead.

distros-with-images.comment: Only Ubuntu is published as a board-specific image on BeagleBoard.org's distros page (alongside the vendor Yocto image, which has no matching tag in the schema). Fedora has community attempts; Armbian has community discussion but no first-class image; Debian builds exist as community/CI artifacts. Not listed here per RESEARCH.md (only board-specific officially-published images).

bootloader.comment: U-Boot is the bootloader for the BeagleV-Ahead, consistent with TH1520 boards generally.

long-term-support.comment: BeagleBoard.org Foundation has historically maintained boards for many years (BeagleBone Black is a clear precedent), but no explicit support window is published for the BeagleV-Ahead specifically.

android-support.comment: No official Android image is listed on the BeagleBoard distros page. Other TH1520 boards (e.g., Sipeed LicheePi 4A) have Android images, but those are not BeagleV-Ahead-specific.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Launch MSRP USD 149 (2023). Single SKU; price has stayed near this point at distributors as of 2026-04-26.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Single SKU only — base equals top.

available-regions.comment: Distributed via Seeed (China/global), Farnell/Newark (NA/UK/EU), and India resellers (Fab.to.Lab). Effectively global through the Newark/Farnell network.

typical-lead-time.comment: In stock at Seeed (China warehouse) as of 2026-04-26; some regional resellers (e.g., Fab.to.Lab India) showed out-of-stock on the same date.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive official docs portal with introduction, design specifications, and schematics; PDF design files plus open repository on git.beagleboard.org. Slightly below 5 because TH1520 software docs are still catching up to BeagleBone-class polish.

community-size.comment: BeagleBoard community is large overall, but BeagleV-Ahead specifically is a niche/early-RISC-V product within that community — sized as small per the RESEARCH.md guideline thresholds.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run forum.beagleboard.org has active BeagleV-Ahead threads and a dedicated subforum.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Reuses the mature BeagleBone P8/P9 cape ecosystem (electrically, with 3.3V-only caveat) and the mikroBUS shuttle ecosystem; no BeagleV-Ahead-specific accessory line. Mid rating reflects the inherited, not native, ecosystem.
